Ampelopsis littoralis is a woody vine native to China, also known as the coastal grapevine. The bioactive constituents of Ampelopsis littoralis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and stilbenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The classification of Ampacus littoralis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the marine snail community.
